name: Terminal
description: Give Claude terminal, filesystem, and background-job access on your local Linux machine.
long_description:
This extension lets Claude run shell commands, read and write files, and launch long-running background jobs on your local Linux machine. Commands run via `bash -lc` so venv activation, pipes, and aliases work the same as in a real terminal. Output is capped at 100KB per stream with full transcripts persisted to /tmp/claude-term-mcp/runs/. Background jobs are detached and track their status in /tmp/claude-term-mcp/jobs/.

**Security:** Installing this extension grants Claude unrestricted shell access, equivalent to giving someone SSH into your user account. A minimal built-in denylist blocks a handful of obviously-destructive patterns (`rm -rf /`, fork bombs, `dd` / `mkfs` on raw disk devices), but this is a last-resort safety net, not a sandbox. Do not install on machines where Claude should not have unrestricted shell access.

tools.name: run_command
tools.description: Run a shell command via bash -lc. Returns stdout, stderr, exit_code. Output capped at 100KB per stream; full transcript saved to log_path.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a text file with optional offset/limit line-range slicing.

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List entries in a directory with type, size, and mtime.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Create or overwrite a text file. Parent directories are created.

tools.name: run_background
tools.description: Start a long-running command in the background. Returns a job_id.

tools.name: read_background
tools.description: Read status and last N lines of stdout/stderr for a background job.

tools.name: list_background
tools.description: List all background jobs (running, exited, killed).

tools.name: kill_background
tools.description: Terminate a running background job (SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after 5s).

default_cwd.title: Default working directory
default_cwd.description: Folder where shell commands run when Claude doesn't specify a working directory. Typically your main projects folder. Leave empty to default to your home directory.
